message:
fix(query): handle MySQL CONCAT operator compatibility

MySQL uses the CONCAT() function rather than the || operator for string
concatenation (|| in MySQL is actually logical OR by default, or depends
on sql_mode=PIPES_AS_CONCAT being set).

The binary expression builder now detects when:
1. The operator is BinaryOp::Concat
2. The target dialect is MySQL

And emits CONCAT(left, right) instead of "left || right".

This ensures portable query building across PostgreSQL (which uses ||)
and MySQL (which uses CONCAT()) without requiring dialect-specific code
in the application layer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
